x.name: alphaXiv
x.bio: High fidelity research

tweets.text:
read more:
https://t.co/lf5IPuEuAF

Instead of asking an LLM judge for one broad score, this paper decomposes evaluation into many atomic yes or no questions that are easier to verify and debug.

This paper proposed a meta-prompt that turns the task requirements into fine-grained checks, then an evaluator answers each question independently and aggregates them into interpretable scores across dimensions like coherence, consistency, and relevance.

So evaluation doesn't just say an output is bad, it also reveals exactly which requirement failed and why.

Across SummEval, Topical-Chat and QAGS, BINEVAL matches or beats strong baselines like UniEval and G-Eval, with especially strong results on factual consistency and hallucination detection.

read more:
https://t.co/KDwNlwJbBl

tweets.text:
What’s underappreciated is that the rise of strong open-weight models like GLM will actually strengthen Google’s position.

more companies will now start experimenting with fine tuning open-weight models such as GLM and the value will accrue to the infra. 

enterprises want the flexibility to run and fine-tune open models on a managed platform with enterprise-grade reliability, security, and support. And Google Cloud is well positioned there.

also don’t forget Google owns much of the compute stack.

tweets.text: Code: https://t.co/p9pVJSNy60

I built a Chrome extension that turns your "read later" list into dedicated reading time on your calendar.  

Save 5 articles → it auto-books a 30-min "reading block" on your Google calendar, links included. So you'll actually sit down and go through them.  

No account, no server, everything local. Open source (link below)

"The market value of writing has gone way up" (per a PM at Anthropic)

Good writing/clear articulation is required for both building a product (being able to steer models effectively) and building an audience

Writing well has historically been undervalued in tech. Time to change that

x.name: Nikunj Kothari
x.bio: partner @fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire @meter, @opendoor, @atlassian & others. love @shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧

tweets.text:
Follower count has frankly never been the goal. This is why I tweet whenever I like. I was under 2k two years ago and I joined this platform in 2009. 

There are no hacks for engagement, and whenever anyone asks what should I do to increase presence on X, this is what I send. https://t.co/qxJ5t08Uvb

Not sure why 40k follow my random thoughts here on Twitter (fine X), but thank you to each one of you.. 

I tend to randomly oscillate between long form writing, shower thoughts on tech, community building & snippets of family life. If you are expecting only one, there are plenty of significantly better accounts. 

Some of my closest personal and professional friendships have originated because of X. A lot of them through cold DMs. I wouldn’t be professionally where I am without this platform tbh. 

No matter how many wild changes “sink in” over the years, this platform continues to embody the word anti fragile.

For more research on how we built Devin Fusion, including examples, check out our blog:

https://t.co/eHJGf3IwNf

Second, Fusion routes dynamically mid-session.

Engineering tasks often reveal their difficulty late: a “simple bug” might become a multi-file race condition investigation. Fusion can shift work back toward the smarter model when needed, including around context-compaction moments where switching can be much cheaper.

tweets.text:
WhatsApp may be the statistically best app we've ever made.

It's a product manager's dream with its unparalleled addictiveness (DAU / MAU) of 87% and stickiness (M1 retention) of 86%, both #1 in the world while having the #1 most monthly active users for any non preinstalled app of 2.7B users.

Here are the top 25 most used apps in the world by MAU on both these metrics. Some surprising observations:
— There are now 15 1B+ user apps in the world, 8 by Google, 4 by Meta.
— The 3 that aren't are TikTok, Telegram (!) and ChatGPT
— Telegram has more users than Spotify, Pinterest, Netflix, Amazon, Snap!
— ChatGPT's one month retention is #5 after WA, Instagram, Chrome and Youtube. 2yrs ago, it was been #20 by M1 retention
— Shopee, a shopping app in southeast Asia, is huge and retains users better than Amazon!

Useful way to break down consumer businesses especially within certain categories (X vs Reddit vs Threads is a good one). It's shocking how few new apps have been able to break through in the past 10yrs.
